questions.question: The main benefit of multi-agent systems:
options: Parallelism + specialisation -- complex tasks decomposed to specialist agents
questions.explanation: Decomposition reduces context length and allows specialised agents to excel.

questions.question: The supervisor pattern uses:
options: One orchestrator LLM that routes tasks to specialist sub-agents and synthesises results
questions.explanation: Supervisor decides which agent handles each subtask based on current state.

questions.question: Parallel execution in multi-agent systems is useful when:
options: All tasks depend on each other
Subtasks are independent -- e.g. searching different databases simultaneously
questions.explanation: Independent subtasks benefit from parallel execution.

questions.question: Handoff between agents occurs when:
options: One agent passes the task to another specialist better suited for the current need
questions.explanation: 'I need legal advice' routes from customer service to legal specialist agent.

questions.question: The debate pattern in multi-agent systems:
options: Uses multiple agents generating answers and critiquing each other to converge on higher quality
questions.explanation: Agent A proposes, Agent B critiques, iteration improves accuracy.

questions.question: Agent coordination communication happens via:
options: Shared state (LangGraph), message queues, or direct handoff in conversation thread
questions.explanation: LangGraph: shared TypedDict state. CrewAI: task context. OpenAI: thread messages.

questions.question: The key challenge in multi-agent systems is:
options: Coordination overhead, error propagation, debugging across agents, and cost management
questions.explanation: Each agent adds latency and cost. Errors cascade. Observability is harder.

questions.question: Token efficiency in multi-agent systems is improved by:
options: Giving all agents the full conversation
Routing only relevant context to each agent -- each sees only what it needs
questions.explanation: Specialist agents with focused context are faster and cheaper.

questions.question: A memory bank shared across agents typically uses:
options: A vector database (ChromaDB, Pinecone) for semantic retrieval across agents
questions.explanation: Shared long-term memory enables agents to build on each other's findings.

questions.question: Evaluation of multi-agent systems requires:
options: End-to-end task success metrics + per-agent quality + overall latency/cost
questions.explanation: Multi-agent evals test both individual agent quality and overall completion.
